default_creds: POC mode: admin@nvidia.com / any-password (auth checks disabled)
misconfig_patterns: POC mode ships with explicit doc: 'POC does not perform authorization checks on its APIs'
BYOC (Bring Your Own Code) is on-by-default in POC — arbitrary Python execution surface
Auth enforced at TLS layer only, not at route layer — POC Overseer leaks project name + server endpoints + role inventory unauth
DEBUG short-circuit on /promote allows attacker-controlled HA failover hijack

active_probe.false_positive_check: Response must be JSON with sp_end_point field — uniquely NVFlare Overseer

deployment_tells: Port 8002 FL gRPC, 8003 Admin TCP, 443 Dashboard (HTTP fallback if no cert), 8443 dev
2.7 added single-port consolidation — port signal will fragment going forward
TLS root is provision-tool self-signed (no NVIDIA public root)
Heavy medical-imaging consortia: MGH, Mayo, Owkin, Rhino Health
MONAI / Clara Holoscan / Triton Inference Server co-deployment (Triton 8002 collides — expect remaps)
pivot_paths: Dashboard /nvflare-dashboard/api/v1 -> startup-kit download surface (kit theft = client impersonation)
POST /api/v1/promote (DEBUG short-circuit) -> HA failover hijack — DO NOT CROSS
vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-24178 (CVSS 9.8) — authz-bypass on Dashboard, patched 2.7.2 April 2026
CVE-2026-24204 — class TBD, listed in NVIDIA April-2026 bulletin
POC mode is by-design unauth and operators commonly demo-deploy + never rotate
